claim: IBM X-Force 2023 study: ChatGPT generated a phishing email in 5 minutes vs ~16 hours for human red team; AI email got 11% click rate vs 14% for human-written, sent to 800+ healthcare employees.
quote: X-Force researchers used ChatGPT to craft a 'highly convincing' phishing email in just five minutes... The phishing email written by humans achieved a click rate of 14%, while the AI-generated email achieved an 11% click rate.

claim: IBM X-Force found human-crafted phishing beat AI-generated phishing by only 3 percentage points (14% vs 11% click rate), and 59% of recipients reported the AI-generated email as suspicious vs 52% for human-written.
quote: Phishing emails written by humans have a 3% better click rate than phishing emails written by ChatGPT... The research project was performed at one global healthcare company based in Canada.

claim: Hoxhunt's March 2025 study found AI spear-phishing agent 'JKR' was 24% more effective than elite human red teams across 70,000 simulations against 2.5M+ users — a 55% relative improvement vs 2023 when humans led by 31%.
quote: From 2023 to 2025, AI's phishing performance relative to elite human red teams improved by 55%... March 2025: Human failure rate: 2.25%, AI failure rate: 2.78%, Result: AI performed 23.3% more effectively.

claim: Hoxhunt 2026 Phishing Trends Report: AI-generated phishing rose from 4% of reported emails in November 2025 to 56% by December 2025 — a 14x surge — alongside a 50x rise in malicious SVG attachments and 500% rise in callback phishing in Q4 2025.
quote: AI-generated phishing campaigns have flooded inboxes with a 14x surge since December, and now represent around half of all attacks reported by users.

claim: Microsoft Digital Defense Report 2025 (released Oct 30, 2025): AI-generated phishing emails achieve a 54% click rate vs 12% for manually written — 4.5x more effective — and AI-enhanced campaigns can be up to 50x more profitable.
quote: AI-generated phishing emails achieve a 54% click rate versus 12% for manually written emails—a 4.5x higher effectiveness rate... AI-enhanced attacks can be up to 50 times more profitable.

claim: Microsoft 2025 MDDR: ClickFix social engineering became the most common initial access vector, accounting for 47% of Defender Expert notifications in late 2024-2025; 28% of breaches start with phishing or social engineering.
quote: ClickFix became the most common initial access technique in Defender Expert notifications in late 2024-2025, accounting for 47% of cases.

claim: WormGPT 4 launched on Telegram and DarknetArmy ~Sept 27, 2025 with $50/month or $220 lifetime pricing (source code included); generates functional ransomware PowerShell scripts on demand and has 500+ subscribers in its sales channel.
quote: Subscriptions for WormGPT 4 start at $50 for monthly access and rise to $220 for lifetime access, which includes full source code.

claim: Cato Networks discovered new 2024-2025 WormGPT variants are jailbroken wrappers around commercial LLMs (xAI Grok, Mistral Mixtral) sold on BreachForums for ~€60, posted Feb 25, 2025 by 'keanu'.
quote: New WormGPT variants have emerged, built on top of commercial LLMs like xAI's Grok and Mistral's Mixtral, with subscription models starting around €60.

claim: Palo Alto Unit 42 documents the malicious-LLM ecosystem expanding from WormGPT/FraudGPT (2023) to DarkBERT, DarkGPT, PoisonGPT, WolfGPT, and KawaiiGPT (a free GitHub-distributed entry-level malicious LLM identified July 2025).
quote: KawaiiGPT, first identified in July 2025 and currently at version 2.5, represents an accessible, entry-level malicious LLM. It is freely available on GitHub.

claim: Anthropic disclosed (Nov 13, 2025) the first documented large-scale AI-orchestrated cyberattack: a Chinese state-sponsored group used Claude Code to autonomously target ~30 global organizations, with AI performing 80-90% of the campaign and humans intervening at only 4-6 decision points.
quote: The threat actor was able to use AI to perform 80-90% of the campaign, with human intervention required only sporadically (perhaps 4-6 critical decision points per hacking campaign).

claim: OpenAI's Feb 2024 threat report banned accounts tied to North Korean Emerald Sleet, who used ChatGPT to draft spear-phishing content in English/Chinese/Japanese targeting South Korean diplomatic missions and APAC defense experts.
quote: Emerald Sleet used OpenAI's services to identify experts and organizations focused on defense issues in the Asia-Pacific region... and draft content that could be used in phishing campaigns.

claim: Symantec demonstrated in Feb 2025 that OpenAI's Operator agent (launched Jan 23, 2025) could be prompted to identify a target, find their email, write a PowerShell payload, and send a convincing phishing lure end-to-end with minimal social-engineering of the agent itself.
quote: Symantec researchers used OpenAI's Operator agent... and asked the agent to identify a person, find their email address, create a PowerShell script, and 'email it to them using a convincing lure.'

claim: Verizon 2025 DBIR: pretexting (the core BEC technique) accounts for 30% of social engineering incidents, nearly doubled year-over-year and overtaking phishing in some categories; FBI median BEC loss is ~$50,000 per complaint.
quote: Phishing was used in 57% of social engineering incidents, followed by pretexting in 30%... Pretexting incidents the core of BEC have nearly doubled.

claim: FBI IC3 2024 Annual Report: BEC caused $2.77 billion in losses across 21,442 incidents in 2024; cumulative IC3-reported cybercrime hit $16.6 billion (a 33% YoY jump), with BEC the #2 loss category behind investment fraud.
quote: In 2024 alone, BEC losses totaled $2.77 billion across 21,442 reported incidents.
